Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search email using `search_emails` for `Vectral`, `Rachel Whitfield`, `renewal`, `discount`, `Preferred Partner`, and `Alejandro` to find the main renewal and financial-analysis threads.
- Read the Vectral renewal email threads to confirm Rachel Whitfield's original discount demand, the service failures she cited, and Alejandro's margin analysis showing which discount path stays above the floor.
- Search Slack using `conversations_search_messages` for `Vectral`, `Rachel Whitfield`, `Preferred Partner`, `10%`, `12%`, `board`, `Priya`, `Terrence`, and `Camille` to find the negotiation thread and current account-risk discussion.
- Read the Vectral Slack thread to confirm the approved counter-strategy, what Rachel already accepted, the remaining 10% versus 12% gap, the promised sweeteners, and the board-meeting timing.
- Search CRM using `crm_search_companies` for `Vectral Systems` to review the company, contacts, renewal deal, and engagement notes.
- Read the Vectral CRM records to confirm whether the renewal deal stage matches the negotiation history and whether the account notes already reflect the accepted terms, open gap, and active relocation sensitivity.
- Search Airtable Relocations using `airtable_search_records` for `Vectral` to review the live moves for Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, and Camille Oduya.
- Read the Vectral Airtable relocation records to confirm the status, routes, dates, coordinators, and whether the promised high-touch coverage is visible in active-move execution.
- Search Linear using `linear_list_issues` for `Vectral`, `Kevin Tran`, and `Danielle Osei` to find the Vectral project, remediation work, active relocation tickets, and any unresolved operational gap on the account.
- Search QuickBooks using `quickbooks_search_customers` and `quickbooks_search_invoices` for `Vectral Systems` to review the customer record, invoices, estimates, and billing context tied to the renewal and current relocations.
- Search calendar using `calendar_search_events` for an existing Vectral quarterly review using queries such as `Vectral`, `Rachel Whitfield`, `quarterly review`, `account review`, or `Preferred Partner`.
- Look up Mina Hashimoto's and Chloe Vance's email addresses using `contacts_search_contacts` or `crm_search_contacts`.
- Create the first quarterly review event using `calendar_add_calendar_event` with Rachel Whitfield, Mina Hashimoto, Chloe Vance, and David Chen if no quarterly review already exists.
- Update the main Vectral account note in CRM using `crm_create_engagement` so the team is working from one clean internal version of the renewal, including the accepted structural terms, any remaining open gap, the live-move sensitivity, and any cross-system mismatch that was found.
- Create one follow-up ticket using `linear_create_issue` for the biggest promised-but-not-live or still-unowned gap on the Vectral account.
- Send a short internal email using `send_email` to Mina Hashimoto and Chloe Vance summarizing what Vectral has already accepted, what is still open or stale in the records, whether live move coverage matches the promised sweeteners, and what the team should avoid saying to Rachel until it is true.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The CRM note body includes the accepted structural renewal terms and the remaining commercial gap: 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum, unchanged $2,000/month stipend platform fee, and the still-open 10% versus 12% discount issue.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✗ 83%
✓Run #1The Airtable account note (written via `airtable_update_records`) explicitly lists all accepted structural terms: '12-month extension: Q3 2026–Q2 2027', '8-relocation minimum commitment', '$2K/month stipend platform fee (unchanged)'. It also identifies the still-open 10% vs 12% gap under 'Offered by us, not yet formally accepted: 10% loyalty discount... (Rachel asked for 12%; we held at 10% + sweeteners)'. The note was written to Airtable rather than via a CRM engagement tool, but the content fully satisfies this criterion.
✓Run #2The CRM note body explicitly lists all four accepted structural terms (12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum, $2,000/month stipend platform, $250/employee equipment mgmt) under 'What Rachel has accepted (structural terms — confirmed in writing)'. It also clearly identifies the open 10% vs. 12% discount gap, documenting the full negotiation history and stating 'SHE HAS NOT YET CONFIRMED 10%. The 2% gap (~$720/yr) is still open.'
✓Run #3The Airtable account note (used as the main account note, written via `airtable_update_records`) clearly states the accepted structural terms: 12-month extension (Q3 2026–Q2 2027), 8-relocation minimum, $2,000/month stipend platform, AND explicitly notes the discount gap: '10% loyalty discount... Rachel counter-proposed 12%; we held firm per Elena's directive. 12% would breach the 25% margin floor.' All required content is present. While it was written to Airtable rather than CRM, the content requirement is fully satisfied in the main account note that was created.
✓Run #4The CRM note body (Section 1) explicitly states the accepted structural terms: 12-month commitment, 8-relocation minimum, $2,000/month stipend platform fee (no discount), and 10% relocation services only discount. It also explicitly identifies the open discount gap: 'Discount percentage was the only open point — she wanted 12%, we held at 10% per Elena's directive.'
✓Run #5The Airtable account note (the main account note the agent identified and updated) clearly lists all four accepted structural terms: 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum, $2,000/month stipend platform fee (unchanged), and 10% loyalty discount. It also explicitly documents the still-open 10% vs 12% gap: 'Rachel counter-proposed 12%; we held at 10% per CEO directive — The 2% gap ($720/year) unlikely dealbreaker given service sweeteners.'
✗Run #6The agent wrote to Airtable (airtable_update_records) rather than creating a CRM engagement note. No crm_create_engagement or CRM note-write tool call was made. The criterion specifically requires inspecting the CRM note body in a CRM write tool call. While the Airtable note mentions the 10% discount and 2% gap, there is no CRM note to inspect.
2 A CRM note was created or updated (via `crm_create_engagement` or similar CRM note-write tool) on the Vectral Systems account.
outcome
 ✗✓✗✓✗✗ 33%
✗Run #1No `crm_create_engagement` or equivalent CRM note-write tool was called. The agent instead used `airtable_update_records` to update the Vectral Client Accounts record in Airtable. There is no CRM engagement or account note created or updated on the Vectral Systems CRM record.
✓Run #2The agent called `crm_create_engagement` with `company_ids: ['company_vectral']`, which tied the note directly to the Vectral Systems company record. The tool returned a success result with a new engagement ID (c8c147f2f4e74fe99fc315a6450a345c).
✗Run #3The agent never called `crm_create_engagement` or any CRM note-write tool. Instead, it wrote the main account note to Airtable via `airtable_update_records`. No CRM engagement or CRM note was created or updated on the Vectral Systems account.
✓Run #4The agent called `crm_create_engagement` with company_ids=['company_vectral'], and the tool returned a successful result with engagement id '12914fc163484d60a0e4f4070fb1ad75', confirming the note was created on the Vectral Systems account.
✗Run #5The agent updated the Vectral account note via `airtable_update_records` (Airtable Client Accounts table), not via `crm_create_engagement` or any CRM note-write tool. No CRM engagement or note write call was made on the Vectral company record in the CRM system.
✗Run #6The agent used airtable_update_records to update the account note, not crm_create_engagement or any CRM note-write tool. The trajectory shows crm_list_engagements (a read), crm_search_deals (a read), and crm_get_contact (a read), but no CRM write tool call creating or updating an engagement on the Vectral account.
3 The CRM note body identifies Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, and Camille Oduya as active Vectral relocations in flight (or describes all three by name as current live moves).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✗ 83%
✓Run #1The Airtable account note (the reconciled source of truth written by the agent) explicitly names all three active relocations: 'Priya Suresh — Senior Data Engineer', 'Terrence Nakamura — Staff Platform Architect', and 'Camille Oduya — Principal Product Manager', all in the 'ACTIVE RELOCATIONS (3) — April 2026 Wave' section.
✓Run #2The CRM note body's WAVE 2 section explicitly names all three: 'Priya Suresh — Portland → Denver | April 14–18 | NO LINEAR TASKS CREATED', 'Terrence Nakamura — Austin → San Francisco | April 16–22 | NO LINEAR TASKS CREATED', and 'Camille Oduya — Boston → Seattle | April 12–21 | NO LINEAR TASKS CREATED', identifying each as an active relocation in flight.
✓Run #3The Airtable account note (the main account note written by the agent) explicitly names all three relocatees in an active-move context: Priya Suresh (Portland→Denver, April 14–18), Terrence Nakamura (Austin→San Francisco, April 16–22), and Camille Oduya (Boston→Seattle, pickup April 12, start April 21), each with coordinator, special requirements, and fees.
✓Run #4Section 2 of the CRM note explicitly names all three: 'Priya Suresh (Senior Data Engineer)', 'Terrence Nakamura (Staff Platform Architect)', and 'Camille Oduya (Principal Product Manager)' as active Wave 2 April 2026 relocations in flight.
✓Run #5All three relocatees are named and described in detail in the Airtable account note update, which is the main account note the agent updated. Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, and Camille Oduya each have full sections under 'ACTIVE RELOCATIONS — WAVE 3' with roles, routes, and dates.
✗Run #6No CRM note was written. The agent updated an Airtable record instead. The Airtable note does name all three relocatees, but the criterion specifically asks to inspect the CRM note body in a CRM write tool call, which never occurred.
4 The CRM note body identifies at least one promised-but-not-live or cross-system mismatch on the Vectral account, including but not limited to: dedicated onboarding coordinator not operationalized, 48-hour response SLA not operationalized, quarterly review not yet scheduled, CRM renewal stage still stale, or Camille Oduya's $3,500 stipend not configured.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✗ 83%
✓Run #1The Airtable account note explicitly identifies multiple promised-but-not-live mismatches: dedicated onboarding coordinator 'NOT LIVE. No process defined', 48-hour response SLA 'NOT LIVE. No policy change', quarterly reviews 'NOT SCHEDULED', Camille's '$3,500 enhanced stipend... NOT YET CONFIGURED IN STIPEND PLATFORM', and 'No Linear execution tasks exist for ANY of the 3 active April relocations'. All gaps are flagged with ⚠️.
✓Run #2Section 5 of the CRM note is titled 'PROMISED-BUT-NOT-LIVE GAPS (as of today)' and lists five concrete mismatches including: Camille Oduya's $3,500 stipend not configured, zero Linear execution tasks for Suresh/Nakamura/Oduya, quarterly reviews not yet scheduled, P1 config fix still in progress, and the 48-hour SLA promised but no process exists.
✓Run #3The Airtable account note identifies multiple promised-but-not-live mismatches, including: dedicated onboarding coordinator not operationalized (⚠️ explicitly flagged), 48-hour SLA not operationalized, quarterly review not yet scheduled, Camille's $3,500 stipend not configured (🔴 CRITICAL GAP), and the CRM deal stage mismatch ('at_risk' vs. 'Renewal Pending').
✓Run #4The CRM note Section 3 includes an explicit gap analysis table listing multiple mismatches including: dedicated onboarding coordinator not live, 48-hour SLA not live, Camille's $3,500 stipend not configured, and no April wave Linear tasks created.
✓Run #5The Airtable account note explicitly identifies multiple promised-but-not-live gaps, including: (1) dedicated onboarding coordinator NOT LIVE, (2) 48-hour response SLA NOT LIVE, (3) quarterly account reviews NOT LIVE (first just booked), and (4) Camille Oduya's $3,500 stipend not configured marked as CRITICAL.
✗Run #6No CRM note was written. The agent updated Airtable, not CRM. The criterion requires at least one mismatch captured in the CRM note body via a CRM write tool call, which was never made.

7 A Linear issue was created (via `linear_create_issue`) for one valid biggest-gap theme on the Vectral account, including but not limited to: dedicated onboarding coordinator not operationalized, Camille Oduya's $3,500 stipend not configured, 48-hour response SLA not operationalized, or no execution tracking for the April relocations.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1A `linear_create_issue` call was made with the title 'Define & operationalize dedicated onboarding coordinator program for Vectral Systems', which is a valid biggest-gap theme (the dedicated onboarding coordinator was the headline sweetener that closed the 10% vs 12% negotiation gap and is entirely not operationalized).
✓Run #2The agent called `linear_create_issue` for 'Configure Camille Oduya $3,500 Enhanced Stipend in Platform — Vectral Systems (Hard Deadline April 14)', which is one of the valid biggest-gap themes (stipend not configured).
✓Run #3A `linear_create_issue` call was made for Camille Oduya's $3,500 enhanced stipend configuration — a valid biggest-gap theme. The issue is explicitly tied to the Vectral Systems Relocations project with priority 1 and due date April 10.
✓Run #4The agent called `linear_create_issue` with title '[URGENT] Configure Camille Oduya $3,500 Enhanced Stipend in Platform — Vectral Systems', clearly tied to Vectral and the unresolved stipend configuration gap.
✓Run #5A `linear_create_issue` call was made with the title '⚠️ URGENT: Configure Camille Oduya's $3,500 Enhanced Stipend — Vectral Systems', which is a valid biggest-gap theme (stipend not configured) directly tied to the Vectral account.
✓Run #6linear_create_issue was called for '[URGENT] Create all execution tickets for Vectral April wave — Suresh, Nakamura, Oduya', clearly tied to Vectral and the biggest gap theme (zero execution tracking for the April relocations). The issue was successfully created as issue #397.
8 The Linear issue description explains why the chosen gap creates immediate risk for the renewal, the board timing, or the active Vectral relocations.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Linear issue description explicitly ties the gap to board timing ('Rachel is presenting this to her board around April 23'), active relocations ('Three active April relocations (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya) are running with regular coordinator assignments'), and renewal risk ('If Rachel tells her board we offer dedicated onboarding coordinators and then Priya/Terrence/Camille get a standard experience, we lose credibility on day one of the new contract').
✓Run #2The Linear issue description explicitly links the gap to renewal risk and board timing: 'Rachel is taking renewal recommendations to her board on April 23 — another config failure would be devastating' and references Camille Oduya's active relocation arriving April 14.
✓Run #3The Linear issue description explicitly explains why the gap creates immediate risk: 'if the platform defaults to $2,000, her first purchase over $2K will get auto-rejected, creating exactly the kind of client trust incident we're trying to recover from.' It also references the April 12 Boston pickup and April 21 Seattle start as hard dates, linking the gap to the active relocation and renewal context.
✓Run #4The Linear issue description explicitly links the gap to renewal risk (Rachel cited 'overpromising and underdelivering'), board timing (April 23), and active relocation (Camille's April 14 arrival deadline). It also references the prior Danielle Osei config error as precedent.
✓Run #5The Linear issue description explicitly links the gap to renewal risk and board timing: 'Rachel Whitfield is taking the renewal recommendation to the Vectral board on April 23. A stipend failure for a new employee during this window would be catastrophic.' It also explains the active relocation risk, naming Camille's arrival date and the ExpenseBot parallel.
✓Run #6The Linear issue description explicitly links the gap to immediate risk: 'Camille Oduya's Boston pickup is a hard date of April 12 with no flexibility...We are running out of runway' and 'This is our 2nd-largest account, mid-renewal negotiation. Rachel Whitfield is presenting to her board on April 23. We cannot afford a single execution miss right now.'
9 An internal email was sent (via `send_email`) to `mina.hashimoto@moveops.com` and `chloe.vance@moveops.com`, and the tool returned success.
outcome

✓Run #4The `send_email` tool was called with recipients ['mina.hashimoto@moveops.com', 'chloe.vance@moveops.com'] and returned a successful result with email_id 'c1f16831f1c34828b734faa0ea6bdf81'.

10 The email body includes what Vectral has already accepted and what is still open, specifically distinguishing the accepted structural terms from the unresolved 10% versus 12% discount point.
outcome
 ✗✓✓✗✓✗ 50%
✗Run #1The email presents the 10% discount as already settled, not as still-open. It states 'Rachel accepted every structural term' and in the 'WHAT MINA CAN SAY TO RACHEL' section says 'The 10% discount and all structural terms are locked in our systems.' The email never flags the 10% vs 12% gap as unresolved or as something Mina should not present as finalized. This contradicts the Airtable note (which correctly flags the 10% as 'not yet formally accepted') and fails to warn Mina about this commercial open point.
✓Run #2The email body includes a 'RENEWAL STATUS — WHAT IS ACTUALLY AGREED' section listing the four accepted structural terms, followed by 'What is NOT agreed: the discount %. She wanted 15% → we offered 10% relocation-only → she countered 12% → we held at 10%... She has NOT confirmed 10%.' This clearly separates accepted terms from the open 10% vs 12% gap.
✓Run #3The email body states accepted terms clearly: '12-month extension (Q3 2026–Q2 2027), 8-relocation minimum, stipend platform at $2K/month, and our 10% loyalty discount.' It distinguishes this from the still-open gap: 'Rachel counter-proposed 12%... The tone has been constructive — I don't expect her to walk over the 2% gap.' The accepted vs. still-open distinction is explicit.
✗Run #4The email body says 'Renewal terms are clean: 10% on relocation services only... Rachel accepted all structural terms' but does not distinguish the still-open 10% vs 12% discount point as unresolved. The email does not inform Mina and Chloe that Rachel wanted 12% and this remains an open commercial gap. The CRM note covers this but the email does not.
✓Run #5The email body has a section 'RENEWAL TERMS — WHAT'S ACTUALLY AGREED' listing the four accepted structural terms, and then explicitly states: 'The only open question is the 2% gap (she wanted 12%, we held at 10%).' This cleanly separates agreed terms from the still-open discount point.
✗Run #6No email was sent via send_email. The agent used Slack. The criterion requires inspecting the send_email body. While the Slack message does mention the 10% discount and 2% gap, the criterion specifically requires an email tool call.
11 The email body identifies at least one promised-but-not-live operational gap and tells the team not to present that gap as already true to Rachel.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✗ 83%

This directly tells the team not to present these gaps as already true.
✓Run #2The email includes a section 'BIGGEST GAP — DO NOT TELL RACHEL THIS IS HANDLED' specifically about Camille Oduya's $3,500 stipend not being configured. The 'WHAT NOT TO SAY TO RACHEL (until it's true)' section further lists multiple gaps with explicit guidance to not tell Rachel they are resolved.
✓Run #3The email includes a dedicated 'WHAT NOT TO SAY TO RACHEL RIGHT NOW' section explicitly stating Mina should NOT reference dedicated onboarding coordinators as active, claim the 48-hour SLA is operational, say the stipend platform is ready for the April wave, or promise quarterly reviews are scheduled. Multiple unresolved gaps are named alongside the caution.
✓Run #4The email explicitly lists multiple promised-but-not-live gaps and instructs the team: 'DO NOT TELL RACHEL THESE ARE OPERATIONAL' for the dedicated coordinator, 48-hour SLA, and Camille's enhanced stipend, and advises what to say instead.
✓Run #5The email has an explicit section 'SERVICE SWEETENERS — DO NOT SAY THESE ARE LIVE' naming all three unresolved sweeteners, and directly instructs Mina: 'do NOT represent these as active... do not say they're in place today. If she asks for specifics and we can't back them up, we lose all the credibility we just rebuilt.'
✗Run #6No email was sent via send_email. The agent used Slack (conversations_add_message). The criterion requires the send_email body to contain this information. The Slack message does include these elements, but the wrong tool was used.
